synthesis.summary: TTD is up ~7% today and ~13% over the past week, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. With relative volume essentially flat at 1.01x, this looks more like a broader sector rotation or options-driven momentum than a news catalyst.

ABSI is up 10% today and nearly 30% over the past five days on 1.76x relative volume, but no news is available to explain the move. The overbought RSI (77.4) and elevated volume suggest momentum-driven buying or options flow rather than a fundamental catalyst.

ASAN is up 8% today on 1.32x relative volume, but no news is available to explain the move. This could reflect options flow, sector rotation into software names, or sympathy moves with peers — none of which are visible in the supplied data.

ATAI is up ~22% today on 6x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The sharp gain alongside overbought RSI (76.5) and a 29% 5-day run suggests options flow, short covering, or sector rotation rather than a identifiable fundamental catalyst.

CAR is up 23% today and 64% over 5 days with an RSI of 94.7, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The magnitude and velocity suggest a possible short squeeze, options-driven momentum, or sector rotation, but none of that can be confirmed from available information.

CMPX dropped ~10% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Relative volume is below average (0.7x), suggesting this isn't a high-conviction institutional event — more likely thin liquidity amplifying an order imbalance, options flow, or sector rotation.

CMPS is up 42% today on volume nearly 14x its normal level, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. Given the magnitude and unusual volume, the catalyst is likely a significant binary event (trial data, FDA decision, partnership, or short squeeze), but nothing in the available news confirms this.

ERAS is up 14% today and 33% over the past five days on elevated but not extreme volume, with RSI at 78 signaling overbought conditions. No news was supplied to explain the move, so the catalyst — whether a data readout, clinical update, options flow, or short squeeze — cannot be identified from available information.

INFQ dropped 8.18% on volume 6.6x its average with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Given the stock is still up ~27% over the past five days and RSI remains elevated at 66, this looks like profit-taking or a mean-reversion flush after a sharp short-term run-up rather than any identifiable fundamental catalyst.

ONDS is up ~7% today on nearly 2x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The 5-day gain of 13% suggests sustained buying pressure, but the catalyst — whether options flow, sector rotation, or an undisclosed development — is not visible in the available news.

POET Technologies surged 18.5% on 3.9x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The spike could reflect options flow, short covering, or sector rotation, but none of those mechanisms are visible in the available data.

PLUG is up ~16% on more than double its average volume with RSI pushing into overbought territory, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. This looks like it could be options flow, short squeeze activity, or sector rotation into hydrogen/clean energy names — none of which can be confirmed from available inputs.

SANA is up 8.4% today with nearly double its average volume and a 5-day gain of ~19%, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The action could reflect options flow, short covering, or sector rotation into small-cap biotech — none of which are visible in the available data.

SGHC is up 7.1% today and 11.7% over the past five days on relative volume only slightly above average (1.08x), but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The catalyst — whether options flow, sector rotation, or an undisclosed development — is not identifiable from available information.

SGML is up ~10% today and ~25% over five days with no supplied news to explain the move. Elevated RSI (76) and no above-average volume suggest this could be options flow, short covering, or sector rotation rather than a fundamental catalyst.

SIDU dropped ~19% on 2.3x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The selloff could reflect options expiry pressure, retail momentum reversal, or a dilution/offering event not captured here, but none of that can be confirmed from available inputs.

SKYQ is up 13.1% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Relative volume is extremely low (0.15x), suggesting this is a thin-liquidity bounce rather than a conviction-driven rally — particularly notable given the stock is still down 23% over the past five days.

UMC is up 8.2% today with nearly 30% gains over 5 days, unusual volume (3x normal), and an overbought RSI of 80.8, but no news in the supplied feed explains the move. The surge likely reflects sector rotation into semiconductor/foundry names, broader chip-sector momentum, or options-driven flow — none of which are confirmed by available inputs.

YSS is up 7.85% today and 13.7% over the past five days, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. Relative volume is below average (0.66x), which rules out a broad news-driven surge and suggests the move may be driven by options flow, sector rotation, or low-liquidity price action rather than a identifiable catalyst.

ZNTL dropped 12.88% today (down ~28% over 5 days) with no supplied news to explain the move. Relative volume is below average at 0.93, suggesting this isn't a high-conviction news-driven selloff — it may reflect options activity, peer rotation, or institutional flows not captured here.
